name: USS Abraham Lincoln
area: Northern Arabian Sea — repositioned off eastern Oman coast, ~1,100km from Iran
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, F-35C Lightning II (VMFA-314), EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye, CMV-22B Osprey, MH-60R/S Seahawk
escorts: DDG-121 USS Frank E. Petersen Jr.
mission_summary: Primary strike platform — deep strike into Iran and attrition of Iranian naval capabilities
notes: Day 17: CSG-3 remains primary strike platform for Operation Epic Fury. Repositioned to ~1,100km from Iran's coast (per satellite imagery), expanding stand-off distance after Iranian gunboat harassment of escort vessels. Air wing (CVW-9) sustaining high-tempo strike operations against Iranian military targets. Iran has repeatedly claimed to have struck or sunk Lincoln — all claims denied by CENTCOM. DESRON 21 escorts verified via USNI Fleet Tracker. Up to 8 destroyers operating in the Arabian Sea/Gulf of Oman area supporting CSG-3 and independent NAVCENT tasking. P-8 Poseidons from Bahrain augmenting ASW coverage.
source: USNI Fleet Tracker 3/16, Defence Security Asia satellite analysis 3/15, ABC7 3/12, CENTCOM

name: USS Gerald R. Ford
area: Central Red Sea — repositioned south from initial Red Sea entry point
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye
mission_summary: Strike operations against Iran, Houthi deterrence, Red Sea maritime security
notes: Day 17: CRITICAL UPDATE — Ford transited Suez Canal on March 5 with DDG-96 Bainbridge, now operating in Red Sea (NOT Eastern Mediterranean as previously reported). Non-combat fire broke out March 12 in main laundry spaces — burned 30+ hours per NYT reporting. Two sailors sustained non-life-threatening injuries. Propulsion plant undamaged; carrier remains fully operational. Ford has been at sea 265+ days, approaching record-setting deployment (11 months projected per VCNO Adm. Kilby testimony to Senate Armed Services Committee). Iran's Khatam al-Anbiya headquarters warned Ford's Red Sea position constitutes direct threat. Satellite imagery shows further repositioning to Central Red Sea, increasing distance from Iranian threat envelope. CVW-7 continues round-the-clock strike operations. DESRON 2 escorts confirmed.
source: USNI Fleet Tracker 3/16, Stars and Stripes 3/7, Washington Times 3/12, NYT 3/17, CNN 3/17, Defence Security Asia 3/15

name: USS George H.W. Bush
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye, MH-60R/S Seahawk
mission_summary: Third carrier deploying to theater — expected to backfill Ford's Eastern Med role or reinforce CENTCOM
notes: Day 17: CRITICAL CORRECTION — Bush was NOT in Gulf of Oman as previously reported. Completed Composite Training Unit Exercise (COMPTUEX) off Cape Hatteras, Virginia on March 5-6, achieving full combat certification. CVW-7 flew 1,586 sorties during 28-day workup including 693 day and 682 night arrested landings. Expected to transit Atlantic and deploy to Eastern Mediterranean to backfill Ford's vacated position. CO Capt. Robert Bibeau: 'When they need us — whether for air superiority, strike, electronic warfare, or presence — we are ready to deliver immediately.' Multiple sources (Fox News, Times of Israel, 19FortyFive) confirm expected deployment. Defence Procurement International places Bush in Eastern Med as of ~March 16, but USNI Fleet Tracker does not explicitly confirm arrival. Escort composition not yet publicly confirmed for this deployment. Commanded by RADM Alexis Walker.
source: USNI News 3/6, Navy Times 3/9, 19FortyFive 3/11, DPI 3/16, USNI Fleet Tracker 3/16

name: USS Tripoli
strike_group: Tripoli Amphibious Ready Group / 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit
area: South China Sea — en route to Middle East
aircraft: F-35B Lightning II (VMFA-121), MV-22B Osprey (VMM-265), AH-1Z Viper, UH-1Y Venom, CH-53E Super Stallion
mission_summary: Rapid-response force for Strait of Hormuz — littoral warfare, mine countermeasures, maritime security, potential island seizure operations
notes: Day 17: NEW ASSET — replaces previously reported USS America (LHA-6) / 11th MEU, which has NO confirmed presence in theater. Defense Secretary Hegseth approved CENTCOM request to deploy Tripoli ARG on March 13. 31st MEU (~2,500 Marines, BLT 3/1) achieved full certification in February. Was participating in Iron Fist 26 exercise in Japan. Ship spotters identified Tripoli sailing alone south of Taiwan through Luzon Strait on March 13. USNI Fleet Tracker March 16 places Tripoli in South China Sea en route. Naval News estimates 10-15 days to reach Strait of Hormuz area. MEU brings F-35B strike capability, helicopter assault, and surface warfare assets. Analysts note potential for Marine deployments on outlying Iranian islands near Hormuz to interdict mining operations. Approximately 5,000 total Marines and sailors (ARG + MEU combined).
source: USNI News 3/13, Axios 3/13, Stars and Stripes 3/13, Naval News 3/13, Military.com 3/14, USNI Fleet Tracker 3/16

name: Bahrain LCS MCM Squadron
area: Persian Gulf / Strait of Hormuz approaches — mine countermeasures
mission_summary: Mine countermeasure operations in Strait of Hormuz
notes: Day 17: Three Littoral Combat Ships based in Bahrain operating Navy's first operational mine countermeasure mission packages in U.S. 5th Fleet. Critical asset given confirmed Iranian mine-laying operations in Strait of Hormuz (reported March 10 — U.S. destroyed 16 Iranian minelayers). At least two LCS equipped with mine-sweeping packages. MH-60 helicopters serve as primary minesweeping platform using AN/AQS-20A sonar system.

name: USS Florida
area: Gulf of Oman / Arabian Sea (approx.) — exact position classified
notes: Day 17: Ohio-class SSGN. Carries up to 154 Tomahawk cruise missiles. Submarine operations remain classified — position is approximate/notional. Tomahawk strikes have been a consistent feature of Operation Epic Fury including the March 14 Kharg Island raid (90 military targets destroyed per CENTCOM). CENTCOM has confirmed >7,000 targets struck across Iran since Feb 28 and >15,000 by March 13 per SecDef Hegseth. SSGN platform consistent with sustained Tomahawk expenditure at this scale.
source: CENTCOM statements, classification prevents specific confirmation

name: USS John Finn
area: Arabian Sea — operating with CSG-3 destroyer screen
mission_summary: Air defense, escort operations, strike support
notes: Day 17: CORRECTION — ABC7 reporting (citing military sources) lists John Finn among 8 destroyers operating in the Arabian Sea area supporting CSG-3, NOT detached to Red Sea as previously reported. With Ford now in the Red Sea with its own escort screen (Bainbridge, Mahan, Churchill, Hudner), John Finn appears to be operating in the Arabian Sea/Gulf of Oman supporting Lincoln's expanded destroyer screen.
detachment_note: Previous Red Sea detachment assessment revised — now assessed as operating with CSG-3 destroyer screen in Arabian Sea
